commit.message:
fix: preserve Next.js history state in URL sync effect

The game URL sync effect was calling replaceState(null, ...) on initial
room join, which destroyed the Next.js __N metadata on the /#room history
entry. When the user pressed back from /#room/playing, Next.js saw null
state and returned early from its popstate handler without calling
beforePopState — so the forfeit dialog never appeared.

Fix: only replaceState when transitioning from game to lobby (URL still
has /playing suffix), and preserve window.history.state so the Next.js
routing metadata stays intact.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Task 13: Implement text rendering pipeline for VulkanRenderer

- Create VkPipeline for text rendering with vec2 position + vec2 texcoord
  vertex input, 80-byte push constants (mat4 projection + vec4 textColor),
  alpha blending, dynamic viewport/scissor, and dynamic rendering
- Create VkDescriptorSetLayout and VkDescriptorPool for glyph atlas

Co-authored-by: takasaki404 <193405421+takasaki404@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(auth): wrap auth commands in block_in_place to avoid nested runtime panic

The OAuth device code flow uses reqwest::blocking::Client, which creates
an internal Tokio runtime. When called from within the #[tokio::main]
async context, dropping that nested runtime on a worker thread panics
with 'Cannot drop a runtime in a context where blocking is not allowed'.

Wrap the auth command dispatch in tokio::task::block_in_place() so the
blocking I/O (OAuth HTTP calls and dialoguer prompts) can execute safely
on the current worker thread.

commit.message:
fix(gb-apu): correct register reads when off, sweep overflow, and negate quirk (#2037)

* fix(gb-apu): correct register reads when off, sweep overflow, and negate quirk

Fix three APU emulation bugs to make 6 more Blargg dmg_sound/cgb_sound
integration tests pass without introducing regressions:

1. Register reads when powered off (test_01 dmg+cgb):
   - Removed early-return that returned 0xFF for all NR10–NR25 addresses
     when powered off. Each register now returns its correct cleared value
     with proper unused-bit masking (e.g. NR10 → 0x80, NR12 → 0x00).

2. Sweep overflow with shift=0 (test_04 dmg+cgb):
   - clock_sweep() previously skipped the overflow check when sweep_shift==0.
     Per hardware spec the overflow check fires regardless of shift; only the
     frequency register update is gated on shift > 0. Restructured the
     overflow/update logic accordingly.

3. Negate-used quirk (channel1):
   - Added negate_used: bool field to Channel1.
   - Set whenever a negate-mode sweep calculation fires.
   - Cleared on trigger.
   - write_nr10() disables the channel if negate mode was used since the last
     trigger and NR10 now clears the negate bit.

Also removes #[ignore] from dmg/cgb test_01, test_04, and test_07 (all now
passing), and adds length_en() public getters to all four channels.

Adds 3 new unit tests:
- test_registers_return_cleared_values_when_powered_off (mod.rs)
- test_clock_sweep_disables_channel_on_overflow_when_shift_zero (channel1.rs)
- test_disabling_negate_after_calculation_disables_channel (channel1.rs)

Pre-existing clippy warning in mapper256.rs (unused NametableLayout import)
is unrelated to this branch and exists on main.

Closes #2034

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(gb-apu): update dmg_bus test to reflect correct powered-off register reads

test_apu_registers_return_ff_when_powered_off was testing the old incorrect
behaviour (returning 0xFF for all APU registers when powered off). The
correct DMG hardware behaviour is to return the cleared register value
(0x00 for NR50/NR51). Updated the test and renamed it accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove invalid --target-port flag — CLI arg never existed

ROOT CAUSE: 'az cognitiveservices agent create --target-port 8088' returns
'ERROR: unrecognized arguments: --target-port 8088' (exit code 2). This
flag does NOT exist in the Azure CLI cognitiveservices extension.

IMPACT: Every deploy since inception has failed at the PRIMARY CLI create
path, falling through to the SDK fallback (deploy_agent.py). The SDK
creates agent versions but cannot configure the Container App networking.
The Container App's ingress was never properly set up, causing the
persistent RequestTimedOut / client_disconnect errors.

FIX:
- Remove --target-port 8088 from CLI agent create (flag doesn't exist)
- Add agent delete before create to force fresh Container App provisioning
- Port 8088 auto-configured from Dockerfile EXPOSE directive
- Remove invalid safety-net create that also used --target-port
- Keep SDK fallback path for resilience

CI evidence (run #131): Both CLI create commands failed with exit code 2.
Smoke test: 5/5 attempts timed out at 100s (client_disconnect).

commit.message:
fix(ci): switch macOS to universal2 on macos-latest; fix aarch64 server binary linker (#43)

macos-13 (Intel) has been retired by GitHub — x86_64 macOS jobs were
immediately cancelled. The previous aarch64 server binary build used
--target aarch64-apple-darwin which triggered PyO3 cross-compile
detection even on a native aarch64 runner, causing unresolved Python
symbols at link time.

Changes:
- Replace x86_64 + aarch64 matrix with a single universal2 build on
  macos-latest; one wheel covers both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.
- Build server binary natively (no --target) so PyO3 uses the
  platform-native code path and resolves the Python framework correctly.
  The bundled binary is aarch64; Intel Macs run it via Rosetta 2.

access, codemod execution, rename operations, workspace editing, and cache
management) into five cohesive interfaces:

- CodemodSemanticProvider: read-only semantic analyzer access
- CodemodTransformExecutor: file-transforming codemod execution
- CodemodRenameOperations: rename validate/plan/execute lifecycle
- CodemodWorkspaceEditor: workspace edit application
- CodemodCacheController: query cache invalidation

CodemodEngine becomes a composite extending all five interfaces, preserving
backwards compatibility. Consumers updated to depend on the minimal interface:

- naming-convention-codemod: narrowed from CodemodEngine to specific
  intersections (CodemodSemanticProvider & CodemodRenameOperations, etc.)
- Test mock ValidationDelayEngine: removed 4 unused stub methods by

Added ISP assignability tests verifying the structural contracts.

commit.message:
feat: implement host-relay MCP bridge with security hardening

Full implementation of the host-relay system — a lightweight bridge that
lets AI agents in sandboxed environments execute simple shell commands on
the host machine via file-based IPC and the Model Context Protocol.

Components:
- hr/policy.py: Quote-aware command policy validator
- hr/spool.py: ULID-based job/result file I/O with atomic writes
- hr/worker.py: Job execution with Popen for graceful SIGTERM→SIGKILL
- hr/watcher.py: Thread pool watcher with fcntl locking
- hr/mcp_server.py: FastMCP server (host_run, spool_status, abort_job)
- hr/main.py: CLI (hr start/status/stop/mcp/--once)
- hr/env_resolver.py: Login shell env sourcing with dangerous key blocking
- install.sh: One-line cross-platform installer

Security hardening (from Opus 4.6 audit):
- Block BASH_ENV, LD_PRELOAD, DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES + 20 more env keys
- Reject $VAR, ${VAR}, $'...' ANSI-C quoting in commands
- Block wrapper commands (env, nice, sudo, awk, strace, etc.)
- Clamp job timeout to 120s max in worker
- Validate job IDs against ULID pattern (path traversal prevention)
- Skip symlinks and oversized files in spool
- Quarantine malformed job files instead of infinite retry
- Resolve redirect paths to catch ~/../../ etc traversal

149 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: simplify URL scheme — room-only hash with three-segment navigation

Remove player name from URL hash. New scheme:
  /              → Welcome page
  /#room         → Lobby
  /#room/playing → In-game

Player name is stored in localStorage on form submit and read back
when joining a room. Direct URL access without a stored name shows
a name prompt overlay.

Navigation flow:
- Game start: pushState to /#room/playing (creates history entry)
- Game end: replaceState back to /#room (no new entry)
- Browser back from game → lobby: forfeit dialog (beforePopState)
- Browser back from lobby → welcome: disconnect (beforePopState)

Changes:
- hooks/use-game-state.ts: Rewrite parseHash for room-only hash,
  add joinRoom/submitPlayerName/cancelNamePrompt, game URL management
  via pushState/replaceState, updated beforePopState for 3-segment scheme
- components/welcome.tsx: Store player name in localStorage, navigate
  to /#room, pre-fill player name from localStorage
- components/name-prompt.tsx: New modal overlay for direct URL access
  without stored player name (focus trap, escape, same form styling)
